title: New settings allows invalid value for max volume
author: low-batt
body: ### IINA Version develop @ commit c5dccbc ### Release Channel Other ### macOS Version macOS 15.7.4 ### Steps to Reproduce - Build IINA from the `develop` branch - Use the `defaults` command to set `enableNewSettings` to `true` - Start IINA - Click on `Settings…` under the `IINA` menu - The _new_ settings panel appears - On the left side of the panel click on `Audio` - On the left side of the panel under `Audio` click on `Volume` - On the right side of the panel look for the `Maximum volume` setting - Notice the text indicating accepted values: `(100-1000)` - Enter **50** for `Maximum volume` - The value is allowed - Restart IINA - During startup IINA displays the following alert: ### Expected Behavior IINA does not allow an invalid value be set for the `Maximum volume` setting. ### Actual Behavior The new settings window allows an invalid `Maximum volume` setting to be set: ### Crash Report or Process Sample _No response_ ### Logs _No response_ ### Sample Files _No response_ ### How often can you reproduce the issue Every time. ### Required Actions - [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues. ### Optional Actions - [x] I tested with the Nightly build and confirmed the issue is not fixed. - [ ] I tested with the latest version of mpv and confirmed mpv doesn't have this issue.

title: Recognize "forced" and "sdh" in subtitle filenames
author: Careless
body:
If I have the following folder structure:

Movie Name (2026)
- Movie Name (2026).mp4
- Movie Name (2026).en.srt
- Movie Name (2026).en.sdh.srt
- Movie Name (2026).en.forced.srt

The only subtitle that will show as [English] in the subtitle selection is 'Movie Name (2026).en.srt'

I have come across people recommended to name their subtitle files this way, and I chose to use it because it seems the cleanest out of all of them.

Is there another way I should be using so that IINA still recongizes that the other two subtitle (sdh & forced) should also be labelled [English] in the subtitle menu, or is this a bug in 1.4.4?

Even weirder, is the 1.4.3 Build 167 nightly release labels en.sdh.srt as [Southern Kurdish].

What is the recommended way of naming these files?

title: RTL language window controls overlap sidebar
author: low-batt
body: ### IINA Version develop @ commit c5dccbc ### Release Channel Other ### macOS Version macOS 26.5.1 ### Steps to Reproduce - Click on `System Settings…` under the  menu - Navigate to `Languages & Region` - Set `Preferred Languages` to a [left-to-right](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Left-to-right_language#Directionality_and_orientation) language - In the `Applications` section add IINA and set it to a right-to-left language - Open IINA - Start playing a video - Click on `Show Video Panel` under the `Video` menu - The side panel appears - The window controls overlap the top of the sidebar The windows controls on top of the sidebar: ### Expected Behavior For a right-to-left language the window controls are on the right side of the title bar and do not overlap the sidebar. ### Actual Behavior When a right-to-left language is configured at the system level the window controls are on the right side of the title bar and do not overlap the sidebar. However when a left-to-right language is configured at the system level and IINA is configured with a right-to-left language the window controls appear on the left side of the title bar and interfere with the sidebar. When the sidebar is not shown, the window controls will interfere with the float on top status indicator (if enabled). ### Crash Report or Process Sample _No response_ ### Logs _No response_ ### Sample Files _No response_ ### How often can you reproduce the issue Every time. ### Required Actions - [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues. ### Optional Actions - [x] I tested with the Nightly build and confirmed the issue is not fixed. - [ ] I tested with the latest version of mpv and confirmed mpv doesn't have this issue.

title: Multiple problems with cache settings
author: low-batt
body: ### IINA Version develop @ commit c5dccbc ### Release Channel Other ### macOS Version macOS 15.7.4 ### Steps to Reproduce - Build IINA from the `develop` branch - Use the `defaults` command to set `enableNewSettings` to `true` - Start IINA - Click on `Settings…` under the `IINA` menu - The _new_ settings panel appears - On the left side of the panel click on `Network` - In the `Cache` section look for the problems described below ### Expected Behavior The cache settings can be used to adjust IINA's behavior. ### Actual Behavior Multiple problems are visible in this screenshot: --- On the `Enable cache` setting… Looking over the macOS `System Settings` I don't see the word `Enable` anywhere. To match Apple's style this probably should be `Use cache`. This is tied to the mpv [--cache](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-cache) option which is not just on/off: > **--cache=** > Decide whether to use network cache settings (default: auto). Should we be supporting `auto`? When you turn off `Enable cache` the other settings are not shown as disabled. Should some of these settings be subordinate settings and disabled when not using the cache? ---- On the `Seconds to prefetch` setting… This is tied to the mpv [--cache-secs](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-cache-secs) option: > **--cache-secs=\** > How many seconds of audio/video to prefetch if the cache is active. This overrides the `--demuxer-readahead-secs` option if and only if the cache is enabled and the value is larger. The default value is set to something very high, so the actually achieved readahead will usually be limited by the value of the `--demuxer-max-bytes` option. Setting this option is usually only useful for limiting readahead. As can be seen in the screenshot above the value is shown as `36,00`. It is not that the comma is in the wrong place, the last `0` is cut off. Increasing the value by one causes it to fit. With values this large are the buttons that increment by one useful? This: `Default 36000` should use a coma just like the value field does. That default does not match up with mpv's default of 3,600,000: ```text low-batt@gag ~$ mpv --list-options | grep cache-secs --cache-secs Double (0 to any) (default: 3600000) ``` The mpv [--demuxer-readahead-secs](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-demuxer-readahead-secs) option that `--cache-secs` overrides: > **--demuxer-readahead-secs=\** > If `--demuxer-thread` is enabled, this controls how much the demuxer should buffer ahead in seconds (default: 1). As long as no packet has a timestamp difference higher than the readahead amount relative to the last packet returned to the decoder, the demuxer keeps reading. > > Note that enabling the cache (such as `--cache=yes`, or if the input is considered a network stream, and `--cache=auto` is used), this option is mostly ignored. (`--cache-secs` will override this. Technically, the maximum of both options is used.) > > The main purpose of this option is to limit the readhead for local playback, since a large readahead value is not overly useful in this case. > > (This value tends to be fuzzy, because many file formats don't store linear timestamps.) The mpv default value matches the mpv documentation: ```text low-batt@gag ~$ mpv --list-options | grep demuxer-read --demuxer-readahead-secs Double (0 to any) (default: 1) ``` --- On the `Seconds to prefetch` setting… In the new code this setting is bound to the IINA `cacheBufferSize` preference: ```swift SettingsItem.Input() .bindTo(.cacheBufferSize) .hasDescription() ``` Searching IINA sources, that preference is **NOT USED** by the backend. In the old settings window that setting is bound to the IINA `defaultCacheSize` preference. That IINA preference is used by `MPVController`: ```swift setUserOption(PK.defaultCacheSize, type: .other, forName: MPVOption.Demuxer.demuxerMaxBytes, verboseIfDefault: true) { key in return "\(Preference.integer(for: key))KiB" } ``` So this setting looks to be **broken** in the new settings window. This is supposed to be tied to the mpv [--demuxer-max-bytes](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-demuxer-max-bytes) option: > **--demuxer-max-bytes=\** > This controls how much the demuxer is allowed to buffer ahead. The demuxer will normally try to read ahead as much as necessary, or as much is requested with `--demuxer-readahead-secs`. The option can be used to restrict the maximum readahead. This limits excessive readahead in case of broken files or desynced playback. The demuxer will stop reading additional packets as soon as one of the limits is reached. (The limits still can be slightly overstepped due to technical reasons.) > > Set these limits higher if you get a packet queue overflow warning, and you think normal playback would be possible with a larger packet queue. > > See `--list-options` for defaults and value range. \ options accept suffixes such as KiB and MiB. As seen in the code from `MPVController` and the mpv documentation, the units displayed should be `KiB` not `KB`. The IINA default is close to the mpv, but doesn't match: ```text low-batt@gag ~$ mpv --list-options | grep demuxer-max-bytes --demuxer-max-bytes ByteSize (0 to 4.6116860184274e+18) (default: 150.000 MiB) ``` ---- Shouldn't the `Show throbber when seeking` setting have an icon like the `Show throbber when paused waiting for the cache`? ---- **MISSING** When I try and play YouTube videos I have trouble. The video starts to play and immediately pauses to fill the cache. Then it starts playing and again ends up pausing to fill the cache. I'm about to test these mpv options to see if they can be used to improve the behavior. From the mpv manual entry for the [--cache-pause-initial](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-cache-pause-initial) option: > **--cache-pause-initial=** > Enter "buffering" mode before starting playback (default: no). This can be used to ensure playback starts smoothly, in exchange for waiting some time to prefetch network data (as controlled by `--cache-pause-wait`). For example, some common behavior is that playback starts, but network caches immediately underrun when trying to decode more data as playback progresses. > > Another thing that can happen is that the network prefetching is so CPU demanding (due to demuxing in the background) that playback drops frames at first. In these cases, it helps enabling this option, and setting `--cache-secs` and `--cache-pause-wait` to roughly the same value. > > This option also triggers when playback is restarted after seeking. That option sounds like it is intended to address exactly the problem I'm encountering. From the mpv manual entry for the [--cache-pause-wait](https://mpv.io/manual/stable/#options-cache-pause-wait) option: > **--cache-pause-wait=\** > Number of seconds the packet cache should have buffered before starting playback again if "buffering" was entered (default: 1). This can be used to control how long the player rebuffers if `--cache-pause` is enabled, and the demuxer underruns. If the given time is higher than the maximum set with `--cache-secs or` `--demuxer-readahead-secs`, or prefetching ends before that for some other reason (like file end or maximum configured cache size reached), playback resumes earlier. I don't know yet if `--cache-pause-initial` will be enough or if I will have to increase the `--cache-pause-wait` setting. These seem like useful settings for IINA to support. ### Crash Report or Process Sample _No response_ ### Logs _No response_ ### Sample Files _No response_ ### How often can you reproduce the issue Every time. ### Required Actions - [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues. ### Optional Actions - [x] I tested with the Nightly build and confirmed the issue is not fixed. - [ ] I tested with the latest version of mpv and confirmed mpv doesn't have this issue.

title: Plugin video-overlay WebView silently never loads, and crashes in JavascriptAPIOverlay.loadFile on Reload All Plugins
author: caraar12345
body:
### IINA Version 1.4.4 (build 168) ### Release Channel Release ### macOS Version 27.0 (26A5368g), Apple Silicon (Mac16,8 / M4 Pro) ### Steps to Reproduce This is two symptoms of what looks like the same underlying problem in `JavascriptAPIOverlay`/the plugin overlay WebView bridge, found while debugging a third-party plugin that uses the `video-overlay` permission (`iina.overlay.loadFile`, `iina.overlay.postMessage`, `iina.overlay.onMessage`). **A) Silent overlay load failure (no crash, overlay just never appears)** 1. Install any plugin with the `video-overlay` permission whose global entry does: ```js var overlay = iina.overlay; event.on("iina.window-loaded", function() { overlay.loadFile("overlay/index.html"); }); overlay.onMessage("overlay-ready", function() { /* mark overlay ready */ }); event.on("iina.plugin-overlay-loaded", function() { overlay.show(); }); ``` and the overlay page's own JS calls `iina.postMessage("overlay-ready", {})` once loaded. 2. Launch IINA fresh (no reload involved) and open any video file. 3. With debug logging enabled, observe `iina.log`: `iina.window-loaded` fires and `overlay.loadFile(...)` is called, but **neither `iina.plugin-overlay-loaded` nor the page's own `overlay-ready` postMessage ever arrives** — for the entire playback session. The overlay WebView appears to never finish loading its content, and IINA never reports a failure either (no `[e]` log lines, no exception). 4. Net effect for the plugin: it can never flip its internal "overlay ready" flag, so nothing is ever rendered into the overlay, with no error surfaced anywhere. Log excerpt (plugin-added debug logging, `[player0 - WebVTT Subtitles]` is the plugin, `[d]` = debug): ``` 14:12:45.754 [player0 - WebVTT Subtitles][d] [event] iina.window-loaded, calling overlay.loadFile 14:12:45.942 [player0 - WebVTT Subtitles][d] [event] iina.file-loaded url=file:///Users/.../Video.mkv enabled=true 14:12:45.942 [player0 - WebVTT Subtitles][d] [loadVttForVideo] called from=file-loaded ... overlayReady=false 14:12:45.972 [player0 - WebVTT Subtitles][d] [loadVttForVideo] found Video.vtt (145994 chars), overlayReady=false 14:12:45.972 [player0 - WebVTT Subtitles][d] [loadVttForVideo] overlay not ready yet, queuing pendingVtt -- no further "iina.plugin-overlay-loaded" or "overlay-ready" lines for the rest of the ~15s session -- ``` **B) Crash in `JavascriptAPIOverlay.loadFile(_:)` on "Reload All Plugins"** 1. With the same plugin active/loaded (overlay previously loaded and player window open). 2. Choose **Plugins → Reload All Plugins**. 3. IINA crashes 100% of the time with `EXC_BREAKPOINT (SIGTRAP)`. This looks like the same class of bug as #6094 (which was fixed for `JavascriptAPIHttp`/timers) but for the `JavascriptAPIOverlay` path: `reloadAllPlugins` tears down the old plugin instance while `EventController.emit` synchronously replays `iina.window-loaded` to the (already-reloading) plugin instance, which calls back into `overlay.loadFile(_:)` on a bridge object that's mid-teardown. ### Expected Behavior - The overlay WebView should either finish loading and fire `iina.plugin-overlay-loaded`, or fail visibly (logged error / rejected promise / thrown JS exception) so a plugin can detect and recover from the failure. It should not silently never resolve. - Reloading plugins (Plugins → Reload All Plugins) should never crash IINA, regardless of which bridged API (`http`, `overlay`, etc.) a plugin is using at the time. ### Actual Behavior - (A) The overlay WebView silently never finishes loading/never signals readiness; the plugin has no way to know anything went wrong, and its subtitle overlay never renders anything for the rest of the session. - (B) Reloading plugins with an active overlay plugin crashes IINA immediately with a native trap inside `JavascriptAPIOverlay.loadFile(_:)`. ### Crash Report or Process Sample
Crash header ``` app_version: 1.4.4, build 168 os_version: macOS 27.0 (26A5368g) exception: EXC_BREAKPOINT (SIGTRAP), codes: 0x0000000000000001, 0x0000000102c0bd00 termination: SIGNAL / Trace/BPT trap: 5 faultingThread: 0 ```
Thread 0 (crashed, main thread) — reconstructed symbol stack ``` 0 IINA JavascriptAPIOverlay.loadFile(_:) (imageOffset 261376) 1 IINA 2 CoreFoundation __invoking___ 3 CoreFoundation -[NSInvocation invoke] 4 JavaScriptCore JSC::ObjCCallbackFunctionImpl::call(JSContext*, ...) 5 JavaScriptCore JSC::objCCallbackFunctionCallAsFunction(...) 6 JavaScriptCore JSC::callObjCCallbackFunction(JSC::JSGlobalObject*, JSC::CallFrame*) 7 JavaScriptCore llint_internal_function_call_trampoline 8 JavaScriptCore js_trampoline_op_call_ignore_result 9 JavaScriptCore vmEntryToJavaScriptTrampoline 10 JavaScriptCore JSC::Interpreter::executeCall(...) 11 JavaScriptCore JSObjectCallAsFunction 12 JavaScriptCore -[JSValue callWithArguments:] 13 IINA EventController.emit(_:data:) 14 IINA specialized MainMenuActionHandler.reloadAllPlugins(_:) 15 AppKit -[NSApplication(NSResponder) sendAction:to:from:] 16 AppKit -[NSMenuItem _corePerformAction:] 17 AppKit _NSMenuPerformActionWithHighlighting 18 AppKit -[NSMenu performActionForItemAtIndex:] 19 AppKit -[NSMenu _internalPerformActionForItemAtIndex:invocationType:] 20 AppKit +[NSCocoaMenuImpl _performActionForMenuItem:invocationType:] 21 AppKit -[NSMenuTrackingSession _performPostTrackingDismissalActions] 22 AppKit -[NSMenuTrackingSession startRunningMenuEventLoop:] 23 AppKit -[NSMenuTrackingSession startMonitoringEvents:] 24 AppKit -[NSCocoaMenuImpl openWithOptions:] 25 AppKit +[NSMenu(MenuBarAgent) _triggerActionForItemID:source:completionHandler:] 26 AppKit -[NSMenuBarItemManagerDelegate menuBarItemManager:triggerActionForItemID:source:completionHandler:] ... 32 CoreFoundation __CFRUNLOOP_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_A_BLOCK__ 33 CoreFoundation __CFRunLoopDoBlocks / __CFRunLoopRun / _CFRunLoopRunSpecificWithOptions ... AppKit -[NSApplication run] → NSApplicationMain → main → start ``` The key detail: frame 13/14 shows this crash originates from `MainMenuActionHandler.reloadAllPlugins` → `EventController.emit`, which synchronously replays a plugin event (`iina.window-loaded`) into a plugin instance whose bridged `JavascriptAPIOverlay` is being deallocated as part of the reload, exactly analogous to the `JavascriptAPIHttp`/timer race described in #6094 and fixed in e5db572 — except that fix does not appear to cover the overlay API's call path.
I'm happy to attach the full `.ips` crash report and `iina.log`/`mpv.log` files if that's useful — didn't want to paste the raw JSON/debug-log dumps inline given their size. Can also share a minimal reproduction plugin on request. ### Logs Available on request (iina.log with plugin debug logging enabled, mpv.log). Left out of this report to keep it reasonably sized — happy to attach if a maintainer wants them. ### Sample Files Not file-format specific — any video + a plugin using the `video-overlay` permission reproduces this. Can provide the minimal reproduction plugin on request. ### How often can you reproduce the issue 100% for both (A) and (B) on this machine/OS build. ### Related - #6094 — same root cause class (plugin instance torn down mid-reload while a queued/synchronous call still reaches its bridged API object), but for `JavascriptAPIHttp`. Fixed in e5db572 for that API; this report shows the same class of issue for `JavascriptAPIOverlay`, plus a related silent-failure mode (A) that doesn't even require a reload to hit. - #5957 — general "plugins can crash IINA" class of issue. ### Required Actions - [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues.

title: Saving a playlist to the same folder as the files doesn't use relative paths to the files
author: anohren
body:
### IINA Version

1.4.4

### Release Channel

Release

### macOS Version

15.7

### Steps to Reproduce

- Save the current queue of files that are all residing in the same folder as a playlist to that same folder
- The playlist references the files with absolute paths that will break as soon as the folder is moved

A relative path to the files should be used when possible. E.g. the greatest common denominator file path prefix (including the future path of the playlist) can be found in order to generate relative file paths, which covers the common case where all files are in the same folder.

Absolute paths are used in the generated playlist file

title: Improvements to plugin update checker: support default branch and flag missing Info.json
author: yorkyang2333
body:
**What you want IINA to do:**
1. Support checking the repository's default branch (e.g., `main` or fetching the default branch dynamically) before checking for plugin updates, rather than exclusively expecting a `master` branch. The plugin documentation should be updated accordingly.
2. Explicitly flag an error when `Info.json` is missing during a plugin update check, rather than failing silently, to inform developers that the repository is not set up properly.

**What IINA does currently:**
- The plugin update checker strictly expects developers to use a `master` branch for update checks.
- If `Info.json` is missing, the update process fails silently without notifying the user/developer.

**Why you think this should be added:**
As pointed out by @lhc70000 in PR [#6160](https://github.com/iina/iina/pull/6160):
- Many modern repositories use `main` as their default branch, so limiting the check to `master` restricts developers.
- A missing `Info.json` means the developer didn't properly set up the repo, which is an error that needs to be surfaced for debugging purposes rather than ignored.

**Examples of other projects that have something similar:**
N/A

title: Crash when removing the last/currently playing item from playlist
author: baioug
body:
### IINA Version

1.4.x

### Release Channel

Release

### macOS Version

macos 27beta

### Steps to Reproduce


必现的bug， 只要你移除最后一项的时候正在播放必然崩溃，这么bug为为什么一直不修复
### Description
IINA crashes consistently when removing the last (and currently playing) item from the playlist. This issue is 100% reproducible.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open a video or add multiple videos to the playlist.
2. Advance playback to the **last item** in the playlist (or have only one item in the playlist).
3. While the video is **actively playing**, open the playlist panel and click the minus `-` button to remove this last item.

### Actual behavior
IINA freezes for a split second and then crashes immediately. 

### Expected behavior
The playback should stop safely, the player should enter the idle state (or close the window), and the app should not crash.

### Environment
* **IINA version:** [例如: 1.3.5]
* **macOS version:** [例如: macOS 14.5 Sonoma]
* **MPV version:** [例如: 0.38.0 - 如果不知道可以删掉这行]

### Additional context
* The crash **does not** happen if I pause the video before removing the last item. It only happens during active playback.
* [If you have a crash log, paste it here or attach the .crash file]


### Expected Behavior

no need

### Actual Behavior

no need

### Crash Report or Process Sample

_No response_

### Logs

_No response_

### Sample Files

_No response_

### How often can you reproduce the issue

100%

### Required Actions


- [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues.

### Optional Actions


- [ ] I tested with the Nightly build and confirmed the issue is not fixed.
- [ ] I tested with the latest version of mpv and confirmed mpv doesn't have this issue.

title: HDR10 HEVC playback shows block corruption + white-flash artifact, absent in raw mpv with hwdec=no
author: NotaBot6565
body:
### IINA Version

1.4.4

### Release Channel

Release

### macOS Version

26.5.1

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a 2160p HDR10 HEVC remux file in IINA (e.g. Fight.Club.1999.REPACK.2160p.UHD.Blu-ray.Remux.HDR.HEVC.DTS-HD.MA.5.1)
2. Play normally
3. Corruption appears randomly during playback, roughly every 10-40 seconds in my testing — not tied to a specific timestamp or scene

Troubleshooting already performed:
- Confirmed not caused by external drive/connection — same corruption occurs playing from internal SSD
- Switched Hardware Decoder setting from "Auto (Copy)" to "Auto" — no change
- Set hwdec=no via ~/Library/Application Support/com.colliderli.iina/mpv/mpv.conf and confirmed via Inspector (Hw Decoder: no) that software decoding was active — corruption still occurred
- Tested the identical file directly in raw mpv (mpv --hwdec=no <file>), bypassing IINA's app layer entirely — corruption did NOT occur. Playback was clean.
- This isolates the issue to IINA's own rendering/HDR-handling layer on top of mpv, not mpv itself, not the decode path, and not the source file/storage
- VLC plays the same file without this corruption, but VLC does not render true HDR for this file (effectively SDR fallback), so it isn't a fully equivalent comparison

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e2b2c12-297a-47e6-a8b8-d8b6017457a0

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d182f745-84ea-48ce-b5e6-71067a986dde

### Expected Behavior

Smooth, correctly-rendered HDR10 playback of the 2160p HEVC/HDR10 remux file with no visual artifacts, matching the clean playback observed in raw mpv (see attached "mpv_clean" recording).

### Actual Behavior

At random points during playback (not tied to a specific timestamp or scene), a frame renders with visible block/macroblock corruption, immediately followed by 1-2 frames that blow out to solid white (losing all HDR tone-mapping/detail), then recovers cleanly to normal playback. This occurs roughly every 10-40 seconds of playback. See attached "iina_corruption" recording — the artifact is visible starting around the 6-second mark of that clip.

### Crash Report or Process Sample

_No response_

### Logs

_No response_

### Sample Files

_No response_

### How often can you reproduce the issue

Consistently — occurs on every playback of HDR10 HEVC content in IINA, at random intervals (roughly every 10-40 seconds). 100% reproducible across multiple playback sessions.

### Required Actions


- [x] I tried my best to search from the existing issues (including closed issues) and didn't find duplicated issues.

### Optional Actions


- [ ] I tested with the Nightly build and confirmed the issue is not fixed.
- [x] I tested with the latest version of mpv and confirmed mpv doesn't have this issue.

title: Upgrade bundled ffmpeg to 8.1.2 to fix "PixelSmash" security vulnerability
author: neuroboter
body:
<!-- Please use English, if possible. If you really feel the need to use a different language to get your point across, you may add a description in your native language to supplement your report. -->
<!-- Please also try to search for your request to avoid it being closed as a duplicate. -->

**What you want IINA to do:**
Update bundled FFmpeg version to 8.1.2 (or later) to fix security vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-8461)

**What IINA does currently:**
Version 1.4.3 of IINA for macOS ships with ffmpeg version 7.0.1

**Why you think this should be added:**
For security reasons.

Source:
https://jfrog.com/blog/pixelsmash-critical-ffmpeg-vulnerability-turns-media-files-into-weapons/

title: "Play next item automatically" option
author: CacheStacks
body:
**What you want IINA to do:**

Add an option to "Play next item automatically" in the _General > Playlist_ setting to only do so if all files in the playlist are audio files.
 
**What IINA does currently:**

Plays next item automatically regardless of the file type.

**Why you think this should be added:**

It would make it easier to listen to an entire music album as well as watch single video files without having to swap the setting back and forth.

**Examples of other projects that have something similar:**

Unsure.
